description: Iraq's invasion of Kuwait triggered fears of supply disruption from the Gulf region and a sharp price spike.
Currency collapse across East Asia sharply reduced regional oil demand pressuring prices downward.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers agreed to cut output after prices crashed to historic lows in 1998.
Terrorist attacks on the US raised geopolitical risk premiums and disrupted global travel and trade demand.
Military invasion of Iraq raised concerns over Middle East supply stability.
Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggered a global recession and the sharpest oil demand collapse in decades.
Political uprisings across the Middle East and North Africa including the Libyan civil war disrupted regional oil output.

event_name: OPEC Decision Not to Cut Output
description: OPEC's decision to maintain production despite falling prices triggered the 2014-2016 oil price collapse.
Agreement paved the way for the lifting of sanctions on Iranian oil exports increasing global supply expectations.

event_name: OPEC+ Formation and First Production Cut Deal
description: OPEC agreed with non-OPEC producers including Russia to cut output for the first time since 2008.
US withdrawal from the JCPOA and renewed sanctions on Iranian oil exports tightened global supply.
Attacks on Abqaiq and Khurais facilities knocked out roughly half of Saudi Arabia's oil output temporarily.
WHO pandemic declaration and global lockdowns caused an unprecedented collapse in oil demand.

event_name: OPEC+ Price War (Saudi Arabia vs Russia)
description: Breakdown of OPEC+ talks led Saudi Arabia to launch a price war flooding the market amid collapsing demand.
Invasion triggered sanctions on Russian energy exports and fears of major supply disruption in Europe.

event_name: EU Ban and Price Cap on Russian Oil
description: EU agreed to phase out Russian seaborne crude imports and later imposed a price cap with G7 partners.
